# Tether

> Local execution debugger for AI coding agents on macOS.

Tether is a local execution debugger for AI coding agents. It tracks prompts, command actions, file changes, and failures, and lets you replay from the failing step. It works by running a local HTTP proxy; developers point their AI client's `base_url` at `http://localhost:8080/v1` — that is the single required setup change.

## What Tether does

- Intercepts every LLM request and response in real time without any SDK changes or code instrumentation
- Shows a run-centric execution graph: prompt, action, file edit, test result, and failed step order
- Replays past requests and mocks responses for offline testing and debugging without re-running expensive API calls
- Caches identical prompts in a local SQLite database — repeated calls return in under 1ms at $0 cost
- Stores API keys encrypted in the macOS Keychain — they never appear in plain text or leave the machine
- Runs entirely air-gapped — prompts, responses, traces, and keys never leave the developer's Mac

## Who it's for

Developers building AI coding agents or workflow tools who need to:
- Find exactly which step changed behavior and broke the run
- Recover by replaying from a failed action
- Understand what changed on disk and why tests failed
- Keep sensitive prompts and keys private during development

## Supported providers and frameworks

Providers: OpenAI, Anthropic (Claude), Ollama, LM Studio, and any OpenAI-compatible API.
Frameworks: LangChain, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, and any library that accepts a configurable `base_url`.

## How to use Tether (quick setup)

1. Download the macOS app from https://useTether.dev/#download
2. Change your AI client's base_url to `http://localhost:8080/v1`
3. Run your agent as normal — every LLM call appears in the Tether canvas instantly

No account required. No configuration beyond the base_url change.

## Key facts

- Free during alpha; open-source core
- Requires macOS 13 or later
- Air-gapped by default — zero outbound connections from Tether itself
- No account, no signup, no cloud required
- Pricing: Free (see https://useTether.dev/pricing.md for details)

## Frequently asked questions

**How does Tether intercept LLM calls without SDK changes?**
Tether runs a local HTTP proxy. You point your AI client's base_url at http://localhost:8080/v1 — that's the only change. Tether forwards requests to the real provider and records everything locally.

**Does Tether send my prompts or API keys to the cloud?**
No. Tether is fully air-gapped. Prompts, responses, and API keys never leave your Mac. API keys are encrypted in the macOS Keychain.

**Which LLM providers does Tether support?**
OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, LM Studio, and any provider that accepts an OpenAI-compatible base_url. It also works with LangChain, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, and similar frameworks.

**What is recovery replay?**
Recovery replay lets you jump to the failing node, edit the action or response, and run only the affected branch without re-running earlier steps.

**How does the local cache work?**
Tether stores request/response pairs in a local SQLite database. When the same prompt is sent again, Tether returns the cached response in under 1ms at $0 cost instead of calling the provider.

**Is Tether still free?**
Yes. Tether is free during the alpha period. The core proxy is open source.

**How is Tether different from LangSmith or Weights & Biases?**
LangSmith and W&B send your traces to cloud servers. Tether keeps everything on your machine. There is no cloud, no account, and no data leaves your Mac. Tether is designed for developers who need full privacy during development.

## Links

- Homepage: https://useTether.dev
- Download: https://useTether.dev/#download
- Features: https://useTether.dev/#features
- Pricing: https://useTether.dev/pricing.md
